travellers.name: Traveller B
travellers.notes: Assume included in general sightseeing unless edited.

travellers.name: Traveller C
travellers.notes: May split off sometimes. Fallbacks included.

travellers.name: Traveller D
travellers.notes: May split off sometimes. Fallbacks included.

transport_legs.notes: Visible screenshot shows one named traveller on the return.

accommodation.name: Tokyo East Base Hotel
accommodation.notes: Hotel confirmation shows 2 rooms for 3 adults.

bookings.action_needed: Confirm whether a fourth adult is attached to a separate booking.
bookings.notes_source: Uploaded hotel confirmation PDF. 2 rooms, 8 nights, east Tokyo base.
bookings.why_it_matters: The base hotel shapes the whole east-Tokyo routing pattern.

bookings.item: Air New Zealand NZ99 AKL -> Narita
bookings.action_needed: Confirm how the other two travellers are reaching Tokyo.
bookings.notes_source: Uploaded outbound e-ticket PDF. Bag drop closes 08:45 NZT, arrival 18:15 JST.
bookings.why_it_matters: Locks the arrival timing and how gentle the first night should be.

bookings.action_needed: Add 2 tickets or keep the split-plan evening.
bookings.notes_source: User screenshot shows 2 adult tickets at 18:00.
bookings.why_it_matters: Creates a fixed sightseeing and shopping anchor for the evening.

bookings.action_needed: Either add coverage or keep the split-plan fallback for the non-ticketed pair.
bookings.notes_source: User screenshot shows 2 adult tickets at 14:30.
bookings.why_it_matters: Shapes the Toyosu afternoon and whether the whole group can move together.

bookings.action_needed: Confirm the attendee pass and where the rest of the group will regroup.
bookings.notes_source: Official event page shows 26 to 27 May 2026 in Tachikawa.
bookings.why_it_matters: Protects the work anchor without pretending the whole group is occupied.

bookings.action_needed: Confirm whether the visible return booking covers only Traveller A.
bookings.notes_source: User screenshot names one traveller on the return itinerary and arrival back to Auckland on 30 May.
bookings.why_it_matters: Sets the end-of-trip buffer and transit planning.

fixed_events.why_it_matters: Protect work time while keeping a nearby family fallback available.
Preserve the work block, then regroup for dinner.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Final admin sweep. Check passports, insurance, eSIM, medicines, spare glasses, adapters, and screenshots.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Quick pharmacy or travel-shop run for anything missing.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Save all booking PDFs offline and sort the airport transfer plan.
daily_rows.notes: Keep the day practical rather than ambitious.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Pack checked bags and carry-on. Put laptop, medicines, glasses, and key documents in carry-on.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Check chargers, camera gear, and travel cards.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Weigh bags and move non-essential liquids into checked baggage.
daily_rows.shopping_focus: Neck pillow, sling bag, light jacket
daily_rows.fixed_booking_time: Air New Zealand online check-in opens 10:15 NZT
daily_rows.notes: Leave room in luggage for return shopping.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Check in online if available, confirm airport timing, set alarms, and keep the night quiet.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Simple family dinner at home and final cash-card check.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Print or screenshot flight and hotel confirmations one last time.
daily_rows.notes: Treat this as a low-stimulation night.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Head to AKL, clear bag drop before cut-off, and depart on the outbound flight.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Breakfast after security if timing is comfortable.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Keep airport shopping to true essentials only.
daily_rows.notes: Keep passports and chargers easy to reach.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Long-haul flight. Rest, hydrate, and keep arrival paperwork handy.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Use downtime to tighten the buy list.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Light conference reading only if energy is good.
daily_rows.notes: Nothing heroic on flight day.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Arrive Narita, clear immigration, reach Asakusabashi, check in, and keep dinner simple.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Convenience-store run and early shower if everyone is flat.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Skip a sit-down meal if jet lag is rough.
daily_rows.notes: Arrival night should stay calm.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Akihabara tech start with a flagship electronics browse.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Smaller camera and gadget stores if the main stop is enough.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Sleep in and start later if jet lag lingers.
daily_rows.notes: Good first full day because it stays indoors and easy.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Move to Asakusa for gifts, snacks, and an easy market lane loop.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Kitchenware street for practical gifts.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Ueno market if the group wants more energy.
daily_rows.notes: Keeps east-Tokyo travel tight and sensible.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Slow dinner in Asakusa and an easy walk back toward the hotel area.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Casual izakaya nearby if everyone still has energy.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Return close to base for the easiest dinner possible.
daily_rows.notes: Nice night for a better meal if the group feels good.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Ginza clothing pass. Start with the high-efficiency flagship stores.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Swap to a calmer department-store loop if the group wants less walking.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Split by store floors so everyone can move at their own speed.
daily_rows.notes: Best high-efficiency shopping day.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Shoes, stationery, and practical-gift sweep while staying in the same corridor.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Shift to a station-area browse if one store finishes quickly.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Department-store split for different gift categories.
daily_rows.shopping_focus: Shoes, pens, art supplies, tidy gifts
daily_rows.notes: Strong day for requester-specific shopping.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Dinner in Ginza or Yurakucho and a short evening walk.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Department-food-hall picnic back at the hotel.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Return early to sort purchases and rest feet.
daily_rows.notes: Keep the evening lighter after a shopping-heavy day.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Light morning close to base with brunch and a short neighbourhood browse.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Kitchenware revisit for practical gifts.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Market alternative if someone skipped it earlier.
daily_rows.notes: Save legs for the Skytree area later.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Use Solamachi as the weather-safe base before the booked evening view slot.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Keep it fully indoors if the weather turns.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Snack stop and character-store circuit if attention spans are mixed.
daily_rows.fixed_booking_time: Be ready for the 18:00 ticketed slot
daily_rows.notes: Good all-weather shopping block.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Booked pair enters the tower while the rest of the group does dinner or riverside views nearby, then regroup.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Buy two more tickets if full-group entry matters more than cost.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Keep the whole group together below the tower if a split feels messy.
daily_rows.notes: First built-in split plan.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Low-friction mall start with casual shopping and simple lunch options.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Swap to a nearby indoor complex if the group wants even less walking.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Sleep in and head straight to Toyosu instead.
daily_rows.notes: Good contrast to the heavier shopping day.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Toyosu lunch, then the booked pair heads into the attraction while the others use the waterfront fallback.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Short queue-dependent meal stop if timing allows.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Mall, waterfront, or cafe loop for the non-ticketed pair.
daily_rows.notes: Second built-in split plan if coverage stays partial.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Regroup for dinner and keep the evening flexible near the bay or back near the hotel.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Simple mall dinner if energy is dropping.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Head straight back to base if the attraction ran long.
daily_rows.notes: Do not force extra movement after the split activity.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Conference day 1 for Traveller A in Tachikawa.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Family uses a nearby park or mixed retail zone instead of commuting back and forth.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: If the family wants an easier day, keep them east and regroup for dinner.
daily_rows.notes: Protect the work anchor.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Conference continues while the rest of the group stays on a simple nearby fallback track.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Family cafe and park loop nearby.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Return east earlier if the west-Tokyo option feels too stretched.
daily_rows.notes: Do not let the family spend the whole day in unnecessary transit.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Regroup for dinner either near the venue or back near the hotel depending on energy.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Quick venue-area dinner if the conference runs long.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Family eats first and meets later if timings drift.
daily_rows.notes: This is the most likely regroup point for the day.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Conference day 2 for Traveller A.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Family keeps the day light and nearby again.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: East-side recovery day for the family if yesterday felt too full.
daily_rows.notes: Protect work time again.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Finish the conference while the rest of the group stays on the simpler fallback.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Park and mixed retail fallback nearby.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Return to base earlier if the family wants a slower afternoon.
daily_rows.notes: Keep the day tidy.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Conference wrap dinner or celebration meal, then reset for the final free day.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Simple regroup dinner back on the east side.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Leave the celebration optional if everyone is tired.
daily_rows.notes: Good moment to check what still needs buying.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Last full free day: west-side shopping cluster for sportswear, gifts, and any missed requests.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Keep it to one flagship stop if the group wants less walking.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Split into two short shopping missions and meet for lunch.
daily_rows.notes: Use this to mop up anything the earlier days missed.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Continue the west-side cluster with one or two strong indoor stops, not five scattered ones.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Shift to a department-store block if the weather worsens.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: One pair keeps shopping while another takes a slower cafe or park break.
daily_rows.notes: Avoid zig-zagging across the city on the last full day.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Final city dinner and a quick sort of what still needs packing before the return.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Early dinner near the hotel if everyone is done with trains.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Keep it ultra-light and start packing after dinner.
daily_rows.notes: Do not turn the last evening into another big shopping chase.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Check out, store bags if needed, and use the morning for only the easiest nearby errands.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Light brunch and a final neighbourhood stroll.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Stay close to the hotel if the group needs a calmer pace.
daily_rows.notes: Return day should stay light.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Move steadily toward the airport with buffer for bag handling and transit surprises.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Simple early dinner at or near the airport if timing allows.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Do not attempt another city stop if baggage or coverage is unclear.
daily_rows.notes: This is not the time for one last detour.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Return flight via Brisbane.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Keep an eye on bag and transit requirements before boarding.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: If the visible booking still covers only one traveller, use the review flags as the final check before airport departure.
daily_rows.notes: Leave generous airport buffer.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Transit in Brisbane and keep the sequence clean.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Short cafe break only if baggage and transit rules are fully understood.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Prioritise immigration or re-check steps if they become necessary.
daily_rows.notes: Transit rules should already be checked before travel day.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Arrive home and keep the rest of the day for the simplest possible reset.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Food pickup and basic unpack only.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Leave all non-essential admin for tomorrow.
daily_rows.notes: No heroic errands after long-haul return.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Early night and only the most essential unpacking.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Quick check that passports, receipts, and medicines are all back where they belong.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Defer everything non-critical to the recovery day.
daily_rows.notes: Return evening should stay extremely light.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Recovery morning. Laundry, basic unpack, and sort purchases into keep or gift piles.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Only do the minimum if jet lag is rough.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Leave any expense logging for later in the day.
daily_rows.notes: Use the extra day exactly as intended.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Close out travel admin: insurance claims if needed, receipts, and trip notes.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Skip the admin if the group needs more rest.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: Only keep the urgent paperwork.
daily_rows.notes: Treat admin as optional unless something time-sensitive matters.

daily_rows.primary_plan: Quiet end to the trip and a check of what worked for next time.
daily_rows.fallback_option_1: Early night and leave reflections for later.
daily_rows.fallback_split_plan: No additional work required if everyone is tired.
daily_rows.notes: Close the trip calmly.

options_bank.why_it_earns_a_place: Useful for the arrival night because it stays close to the hotel and keeps the first evening easy.
High-yield first full-day stop that matches the shopping objectives.
Fast way to cover several low-to-mid-price gift categories without adding new districts.
Keeps the high-priority clothing requests in one efficient day.
Lets the group stay productive around the booked evening slot.
Already booked, but current ticket coverage suggests a split plan unless extra tickets are added.
Booked anchor that changes the shape of the whole afternoon.
Keeps the conference central while still leaving the family a coherent fallback nearby.
Strong last full day cluster for any unfinished shopping goals.

prep_tasks.task: Verify passport nationality and entry or transit rules for each traveller.
prep_tasks.practical_note: The assumptions are straightforward for New Zealand passports, but any other passport needs its own check.
prep_tasks.notes: Start here because the visible bookings do not line up cleanly.

prep_tasks.task: Reconcile who is on which flight, hotel, and attraction booking.
prep_tasks.practical_note: The planner assumes 4 adults, but the attached coverage ranges from 1 to 3 adults depending on the item.
prep_tasks.notes: Use the Bookings sheet as the working list.

prep_tasks.task: Confirm the hotel rooming for 4 adults or adjust the booking.
prep_tasks.practical_note: The current confirmation shows 3 adults across 2 rooms.
prep_tasks.notes: Fix this before any free-cancel deadline passes.

prep_tasks.task: Confirm the conference pass and plan family regroup points.
prep_tasks.practical_note: Protect the work days and decide where the rest of the group spends time nearby.

prep_tasks.task: Check medicine legality and quantities for Japan.
prep_tasks.practical_note: Prescription medicines can be straightforward, but quantity limits and controlled substances matter.
prep_tasks.notes: If carrying prescriptions, get a doctor letter and keep medicines in original packaging.

prep_tasks.task: Buy comprehensive travel insurance for all travellers.
prep_tasks.practical_note: Include medical cover and any activities that matter for the trip.

prep_tasks.task: Review routine vaccines and decide whether any travel-health follow-up is worth doing.
prep_tasks.practical_note: Keep the task scoped to what actually matters for an urban Tokyo trip.

prep_tasks.task: Decide whether to buy extra Skytree and teamLab tickets or commit to split plans.
prep_tasks.practical_note: Either finish the coverage or keep the fallback rows intentional.

prep_tasks.task: Set up Visit Japan Web and keep the details easy to reach.
prep_tasks.practical_note: Use it for immigration and customs where relevant.

prep_tasks.task: Arrange eSIM or roaming and save activation details offline.
prep_tasks.practical_note: Do not discover account-password issues at Narita.
prep_tasks.notes: This also belongs on Pack List as a phone screenshot or note.

prep_tasks.task: Check Brisbane transit handling for bags and immigration.
prep_tasks.practical_note: Only keep this open because the visible return coverage is incomplete and the route crosses Australia.

prep_tasks.task: Complete online check-in and keep airport timing conservative.
prep_tasks.practical_note: Online check-in is not permission to arrive late.

pack_items.notes: Final expiry check belongs in Prep & Compliance.
Make it easy to access on arrival.
Pack enough for flights and hotel.
Only if it materially improves the trip capture plan.
Do not assume existing plugs are enough.
Keep a small amount in original packaging.
Check legality if anything is prescribed.
Trip-saver if the primary pair breaks.
Useful for the flights and cooler evenings.

buy_items.why_it_matters: Simple item that can matter more than expected on travel days.
Cleaner to sort this at home than in transit.
Behaves like a purchase even though it is not a physical item.
Useful for daily carry in Tokyo.
Buy only if it genuinely improves how the trip will be captured.
One of the clearest stated shopping goals.
Specific requested purchase worth protecting in the plan.
Good to keep visible, but not worth wrecking the route for.

sources.used_for: Hotel dates, room count, location, and coverage review.
Outbound flight timing, bag-drop timing, and coverage review.
Shopping-led daily route ideas and destination options.
sources.notes: Weekday labels were normalised to the 2026 calendar.

sources.used_for: 24 May evening anchor and 2-adult ticket count.
25 May afternoon anchor and 2-adult ticket count.

sources.source_name: Japan visa FAQ for NZ passport holders

sources.used_for: Transit review task for the Brisbane leg.

assumptions: The trip is still Tokyo-only even though the return transits via Brisbane.
Traveller A is the only confirmed conference attendee.
The group mostly stays together except where partial ticket coverage makes a split plan necessary.
Shopping priorities are important enough to remain visible across the workbook.
review_flags: Hotel confirmation currently shows 3 adults across 2 rooms while the planner assumes 4 adults.
Outbound flight evidence currently shows only 2 adults.
Skytree and teamLab each show 2-adult coverage, so split fallbacks remain necessary unless more tickets are bought.
Visible return itinerary currently names only one traveller.
The uploaded shopping notes used weekday labels from another year and were normalised to the 2026 calendar.

shopping_objectives.goal: Keep clothing, shoes, stationery, sportswear, and snack gifts visible throughout the plan.
shopping_objectives.timing: Across Ginza, Skytree, and final west-side day

notes_raw: Shopping notes mention weekday labels that do not match the 2026 calendar.
Visible booking coverage differs across hotel, flights, and attractions.
notes_normalised: Shopping-note weekday labels were mapped onto the 2026 calendar before building the daily plan.
Coverage contradictions were preserved as review flags instead of being flattened.
